content:
Member-only story

2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Marques Thomas on UnsplashOverall, the assessment consisted of 3 rounds:
• Round 1: Coding Challenge (100 mins)
• Round 2: Workplace Simulation (~15-20 mins)
• Round 3: Work Style Surveys (~5 mins)
Round 1: Coding Challenge (100 mins)
This round was split into two parts: Traditional DSA Question (40 mins) + AI-Assisted Repository Challenge (60 mins)
DSA Question:
The first question was a git-themed string DP/interleaving-style problem.
Closest LeetCode similarities:
• LC 1092 - Shortest Common Supersequence
• LC 97 - Interleaving String
with an inversion-counting twist.
Difficulty Rating: Hard
AI-Assisted Repository Challenge:
Before starting, candidates are asked to choose a framework/language environment. The available options included:
• Node.js (MERN ecosystem)
• Python (Django)
• Java (Spring Boot)
This was a repository-style environment with access to an AI coding assistant. In theory, this felt much closer to actual software engineering than solving…
